children.value: . A coarser, slot-counting-only measure of dispersion that does not respect angular structure (e.g., is insensitive to whether occupied slots are adjacent or opposite). Retained as a secondary measure; see
for why circular variance is preferred as the primary precision DV.

children.value: are mutually exclusive in normal data (a transition trial cannot also be an oddball), so collinearity in the design matrix is not a concern.

is the running count of oddball trials within the current episode, including the current trial. Included as a control covariate in M4 to absorb the effect of differential oddball exposure across longer vs shorter episodes (a consequence of jittered episode length combined with probabilistic oddball scheduling).
for each trial with a delivered outcome (
Two predictors are centred to make interaction coefficients interpretable as effects at the marginal mean rather than at zero:
minus its per-participant mean. Per-participant centring (rather than grand-mean centring) ensures that cross-participant variation in mean episode count does not bias the slope estimate.
minus its grand mean across all trials in the dataset. Used as a control in models that include within-episode dynamics.
